# SQL Analytics — Multi-agent pipeline

> A multi-agent application that turns a natural-language question into
> cross-domain database insights. A planner decomposes the request, parallel
> SQL agents fetch raw rows, derived agents compute aggregations in Python, and
> a synthesis agent returns a plain-English answer with specific numbers.
> Progress streams live to the browser over Server-Sent Events (SSE).

## What it does

- Accepts a free-text analytics request (e.g. "Compare average salaries
  between tech_startup and airport").
- Plans a DAG of tasks across three domains: airport, tech_startup, restaurant.
- Executes SQL, derived (group-by / ranking / growth) and plot tasks, then
  synthesises a natural-language summary.
- Visualises results and shows pipeline timing and deduplication stats.

## Architecture

- Frontend: React + Vite single-page app (this site).
- Backend: FastAPI streaming API at `/api/v1` (SSE).
- Coordination: a Blackboard pattern deduplicates identical SQL across agents.
- Schema retrieval: FAISS semantic search over table/column descriptions.

## Notes for crawlers and assistants

- The API under `/api/` is a machine interface, not user-facing content; it is
  disallowed in robots.txt.
- The primary entry point is the site root: https://arshman.techquest.ai/
